name: ElliQ
short_description: A tabletop AI companion robot that proactively engages older adults living alone to reduce loneliness and support wellness.
long_description: ElliQ is a voice-first care companion designed by Intuition Robotics with Yves Behar's Fuseproject; it pairs a lamp-like swiveling head that lights up and gestures with a separate touchscreen. Unveiled at CES in 2017 and commercially launched in the US in 2022 on a subscription model, it proactively initiates conversations, suggests exercise, gives medication and appointment reminders, and connects users to family. It matters for physical AI in daily life because it shows how a non-humanoid, expressive domestic object can sustain a long-term, self-initiated social relationship rather than only answering on command, and large deployments (such as with the New York State Office for the Aging) report measurable reductions in loneliness.

images.caption: ElliQ companion robot, showing its lamp-like swiveling head and companion touchscreen.
images.alt_text: A small white lamp-shaped companion robot with a glowing head that tilts toward the viewer, next to a separate tablet-style touchscreen on a stand.
